# Occupancy probe report (M2.5 Tier-1 · confirmed on device, M2.7 Tier-2)

**Date:** 2026-06-11 · **Generated by:** `occupancy_probe` (analytical, CUDA-free) · risk **A2**

> ✅ **Device specs confirmed from a live device run** (`tools/devprobe`, M2.7). Kernel resources (regs/thread) remain an estimate until the M3 prototype kernel (`nvcc -Xptxas -v`).

## Inputs

- **Material:** Al FCC (a=4.05 Å), number density ρ = 0.06022 atoms/Å³.
- **Zone width:** 4.00 Å (= R_cut, M0). A TD zone is a slab of this width across the box cross-section.
- **Kernel estimate:** block_size = 128, 40 regs/thread, 0 B shared-mem/block. *(No compiled kernel yet — M3 will refine via `nvcc -Xptxas -v`.)*
- **Saturation target:** a single kernel launch should fill ≥ 50% of the device.

## Device profiles

| Device | SMs | warps/SM | blocks/SM | regs/SM | smem/SM (KB) | confirmed |
|--------|----:|---------:|----------:|--------:|-------------:|:---------:|
| NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 (deviceQuery 2026-06-11) | 84 | 48 | 24 | 65536 | 100 | yes |
| A100 (GA100) — reference, approx | 108 | 64 | 32 | 65536 | 164 | **no** |
| RTX 4090 (Ada AD102) — reference, approx | 128 | 48 | 24 | 65536 | 99 | **no** |

## Per-SM occupancy of the force kernel

Theoretical occupancy from kernel resources vs per-SM limits (independent of problem size).

| Device | active blocks/SM | active warps/SM | occupancy | limiter |
|--------|-----------------:|----------------:|----------:|---------|
| NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 (deviceQuery 2026-06-11) | 12 | 48 | 100% | warps |
| A100 (GA100) — reference, approx | 12 | 48 | 75% | registers |
| RTX 4090 (Ada AD102) — reference, approx | 12 | 48 | 100% | warps |

> A high per-SM occupancy here does **not** mean the device is busy: that depends on whether the problem supplies enough warps (next section). This is the crux of A2.

## Device fill vs system size — NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 (deviceQuery 2026-06-11)

Single thin zone (width 4.0 Å), per-SM occupancy 100% → device holds 4032 warps at once.

| N atoms | box L (Å) | zones in box | atoms/zone | warps/zone | single-zone fill | zones to saturate |
|--------:|----------:|-------------:|-----------:|-----------:|-----------------:|------------------:|
| 72 | 11 | 2 | 27 | 1 | 0.0% | 4032 |
| 1000 | 26 | 6 | 157 | 5 | 0.1% | 807 |
| 10000 | 55 | 13 | 728 | 23 | 0.6% | 176 |
| 100000 | 118 | 29 | 3378 | 106 | 2.6% | 39 |
| 1000000 | 255 | 63 | 15679 | 490 | 12.2% | 9 |
| 10000000 | 550 | 137 | 72774 | 2275 | 56.4% | 2 |

## Sensitivity to device specs (N = 1000000)

The single-zone-underfill conclusion holds across plausible profiles, so it does not hinge on the provisional 5080 numbers.

| Device | device warp cap | warps/zone | single-zone fill | zones to saturate |
|--------|----------------:|-----------:|-----------------:|------------------:|
| NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 (deviceQuery 2026-06-11) | 4032 | 490 | 12.2% | 9 |
| A100 (GA100) — reference, approx | 5184 | 490 | 9.5% | 11 |
| RTX 4090 (Ada AD102) — reference, approx | 6144 | 490 | 8.0% | 13 |

## Verdict (go / no-go)

- 🔴 **A single thin zone underutilizes the GPU.** At N=1000000 a 4.0 Å zone fills only **12.2%** of NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 (deviceQuery 2026-06-11) (per-SM occupancy is 100% — the gap is purely *too few warps in flight*).
- 🟢 **Batching recovers it.** Running **≈9 zones concurrently** per kernel/stream reaches the ≥50% target (63 zones available in the box → feasible).
- The tiny golden system (N=72) is a **force-correctness fixture, not a performance target** — a single zone there is ~1 warp.
- Small/medium systems (≤~10⁶ atoms) underfill on one zone; very large systems (≥~10⁷) approach saturation on a single zone.

### Recommendation for M3

1. The clustering/conveyor **must process multiple zones concurrently** (batch ≈ 9 for the 5080 class) — a one-zone-at-a-time kernel will leave the GPU idle on typical sizes.
2. Keep the kernel register-light (it hit 100% per-SM occupancy at 40 regs/thread, block 128).
3. Treat zone width as an occupancy tuning knob (≥ R_cut), not just a causality parameter.

## Reproduce / confirm on target

```bash
# Tier-1 (any host, no GPU):
./build/occupancy_probe --date <YYYY-MM-DD> > docs/_meta/occupancy_probe_<date>.md

# On the target GPU: tools/devprobe prints the exact flags to feed back:
./build-cuda/devprobe   # -> occupancy_probe flags: --sm ... --blocks-per-sm ...
./build/occupancy_probe --sm <SMs> --warps-per-sm <W> --blocks-per-sm <B> \
    --regs <regs/thread> --block <block> --date <date> --confirmed "<name>"
```

**Tier-2 (on GPU, M2.7+):** device limits via `tools/devprobe` (`cudaGetDeviceProperties` + `cudaOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor` on a reference kernel); achieved occupancy via Nsight Compute `sm__warps_active.avg.pct_of_peak_sustained_active` on the real force kernel — lands with the M3 prototype.

---

## Tier-2 update (2026-06-12): real kernel measurements — M3 prototype

`tests/test_cuda_cluster.cu` (block 128 = 4 warp-clusters, shared staging 3 KiB/block,
owner-writes, no atomics), measured via `cudaFuncGetAttributes` +
`cudaOccupancyMaxActiveBlocksPerMultiprocessor` on the live RTX 5080:

| instantiation | regs/thread | blocks/SM | theoretical occupancy |
|---|--:|--:|--:|
| fp64 pair math | **56** | 9 | **75%** |
| fp32 pair math | **62** (double↔float conversions add temporaries) | 8 | **67%** |

- The Tier-1 model with these regs reproduces the runtime-API numbers exactly
  (it is the same arithmetic): the **40-regs input was optimistic** — at 56–62 regs
  the kernel is register-limited below the 100% line (42.7 regs at block 128),
  but comfortably above the 50% A2 red-flag.
- Correctness: GPU ≡ CPU clustered ≤ 5.1e-15 eV/Å (N=10 976); fp32 path 1.6e-6;
  compute-sanitizer memcheck/racecheck clean.
- **Achieved** occupancy via `ncu sm__warps_active...` blocked by
  ERR_NVGPUCTRPERM (GPU perf counters need `NVreg_RestrictProfilingToAdminUsers=0`
  + reboot, or root) — deferred, not a CI gate. Theoretical-vs-model match is exact.
- Register pressure is the M4 optimization lever (lower precision of min-image
  arithmetic, fewer live temporaries); zones-to-saturate at 75% occupancy: ~12
  instead of ~9 (same conclusion: batching required).
